\section{Generated Blueprint}

% QEDesk[node=image_membership][kind=definition][status=open]
\begin{definition}[Membership in an image]
\label{def:image_membership}
\notready
For a set $S \subseteq A$, the assertion $b \in f(S)$ means that there is
some $a \in S$ such that $f(a)=b$.
\end{definition}

% QEDesk[node=preimage_membership][kind=definition][status=open]
\begin{definition}[Membership in a preimage]
\label{def:preimage_membership}
\notready
The assertion $a \in f^{-1}(B_1)$ means exactly that $f(a) \in B_1$.
\end{definition}

% QEDesk[node=mem_of_mem_image_preimage][kind=lemma][status=checked]
\begin{lemma}[Element chase through image and preimage]
\label{lem:mem_of_mem_image_preimage}
\lean{mem_of_mem_image_preimage}
\leanok
\uses{def:image_membership, def:preimage_membership}
If $b \in f(f^{-1}(B_1))$, then $b \in B_1$.
\end{lemma}

% QEDesk[node=image_preimage_subset][kind=theorem][status=checked]
\begin{theorem}[Image of a preimage is contained in the target set]
\label{thm:image_preimage_subset}
\lean{image_preimage_subset}
\leanok
\uses{lem:mem_of_mem_image_preimage}
The elementwise lemma proves the subset statement
$f(f^{-1}(B_1)) \subseteq B_1$.
\end{theorem}

% QEDesk[node=flt_coprime_setup][kind=definition][status=open]
\begin{definition}[Coprime residue modulo a prime]
\label{def:flt_coprime_setup}
\notready
The assumption $\gcd(a,p)=1$ says that the residue class of $a$ modulo $p$
is nonzero, hence a unit in the finite field with $p$ elements.
\end{definition}

% QEDesk[node=fermat_little_modEq][kind=theorem][status=checked]
\begin{theorem}[Fermat's little theorem in modular form]
\label{thm:fermat_little_modEq}
\lean{fermat_little_modEq}
\leanok
\uses{def:flt_coprime_setup}
If $p$ is prime and $\gcd(a,p)=1$, then
\[
  a^{p-1} \equiv 1 \pmod p.
\]
\end{theorem}
